summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Official Windows service control for Bluetooth Support Service (bthserv). Microsoft documents the service name, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Bluetooth Support Service
setting.casual_explanation: This is the main Windows Bluetooth support service. It helps Windows discover, pair, and work with Bluetooth devices.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems that never use Bluetooth and where every background service is being trimmed down.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it can remove unused Bluetooth activity, but already paired devices can stop working correctly and Windows may stop discovering new Bluetooth devices.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Bluetooth Support Service `(bthserv)` with a default start mode of `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`. The same Microsoft description warns that stopping or disabling the service can prevent installed Bluetooth devices from operating properly and block discovery or pairing of new devices. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `bthserv`.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Bluetooth Support Service starts on demand with Microsoft's documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Bluetooth support disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows no longer starts the main Bluetooth support service until the mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables bthserv by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal Bluetooth Support Service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists bthserv with a Manual default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves the documented Bluetooth support behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You use Bluetooth peripherals or want pairing and discovery available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You intentionally do not want Bluetooth support on the device.
tradeoffs: Bluetooth pairing and discovery remain available.
The service can still start when Windows needs it.

recommended_profiles.label: No Bluetooth support
avoid_for: Laptops or systems using Bluetooth keyboards, mice, audio, or pairing

states.rationale: Disable the documented Bluetooth Support Service.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally want Bluetooth support turned off.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need paired Bluetooth devices or new Bluetooth discovery.
tradeoffs: Unused Bluetooth background activity is reduced.
Paired devices can fail to work properly and new devices may not be discovered or paired.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Bluetooth Support Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Bluetooth Support Service `(bthserv)` with start mode `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`, while warning that paired devices may fail and new devices may not be discovered or associated.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `bthserv` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the bthserv service name, its Manual default start mode, the Bluetooth behavior it provides, and that it is OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app's service-start-mode action matches that documented control surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Bluetooth Support Service (bthserv); Manual; The Bluetooth service supports discovery and association of remote Bluetooth devices. Stopping or disabling this service prevent Bluetooth devices from operating properly and prevent new devices from being discovered or associated.; OK to disable: This service provides functionality that is useful to some but not all enterprises, and security-focused enterprises that don't use it can safely disable it.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft services guidance page explicitly lists the bthserv service, its Manual baseline, the impact of disabling it, and that it is OK to disable.
